content.text:
<user_query>
Wire Firebase Authentication end-to-end so a real user can sign in to the
Legacy Memories web app and capture a memory.

## Current deployed state (v0.5, GCP)

- GCP project: `legacy-memories-prod` (region `us-west1`, org `theohanaway.com`)
- Web app: https://legacy-memories-prod.web.app  (Firebase Hosting, deployed)
- API: https://memories-api-604302431851.us-west1.run.app  (Cloud Run, public, /health = 200)
- DB: Cloud SQL Postgres `memories-prod` (migrations applied)
- Media: GCS bucket `memories-prod-media` (V4 signed URLs)
- Firebase project: `legacy-memories-prod` already linked, Hosting site exists
- Identity Platform / Firebase Auth: NOT yet enabled in the Firebase console
- API JWT verification env vars (in Secret Manager) are populated with
  Firebase issuer/audience/JWKS placeholders and need to be confirmed once
  Firebase Auth is enabled

## What's missing

1. Enable Firebase Authentication in the Firebase console
   - Sign-in method: email link (passwordless), Google sign-in is also fine
   - Confirm authorized domains include `legacy-memories-prod.web.app`
     (and later `app.theohanaway.com`)

2. Confirm the API's JWT verification config matches Firebase
   - Issuer should be `https://securetoken.google.com/legacy-memories-prod`
   - Audience should be `legacy-memories-prod`
   - Update the Secret Manager secrets if they don't already match
   - Restart Cloud Run service so it picks up new secret values

3. Implement the user → practice mapping (custom claim `practice_id`)
   - Decide between: a small Cloud Function on user create, or a manual
     admin script that sets the claim. Start with the manual script for v0.5.
   - Default: every new user gets a personal `practice_id` equal to their
     Firebase UID, matching the current API contract.

4. Web app login UI
   - Initialize Firebase Auth client with project config
   - Add a sign-in screen (email link or Google)
   - On successful sign-in, store the ID token and attach it as
     `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to all API calls
   - Handle token refresh

5. End-to-end smoke test
   - Sign in as a new user via the live app
   - Capture/upload a photo
   - Verify the photo round-trips: signed upload → DB row → playback URL renders

## Constraints

- Keep it simple (KIS rule from AGENTS.md). No over-engineering.
- Add or extend tests where practical (apps/api unit tests for any new
  validation; apps/web component tests for the login screen).
- Keep coverage policy from AGENTS.md (≥80% lines/statements, ≥70% branches).
- Don't weaken existing tests or quality gates.
- Update `docs/infrastructure.md` if the auth setup adds steps a new operator
  would need to know.

## Where to read first

- `AGENTS.md`
- `docs/infrastructure.md` (current GCP runbook + auth notes)
- `docs/technical-design-v1.md` (auth section)
- `docs/development-plan-v1.md` (current milestone status)
- `apps/api/src/app.ts` (JWT verification + practice_id usage)
- `apps/web/src` (current login surface, if any)

## Deliverable

Working sign-in on https://legacy-memories-prod.web.app for at least one
real account, with a captured-and-played-back memory as proof. Document
the operator steps in `docs/infrastructure.md` so this is reproducible.
</user_query>

Implementing Firebase Auth end-to-end: exploring the codebase and docs, then implementing API, web, scripts, and documentation.

[REDACTED]

Checking DB schema and JWT claims: the API requires `user_id` to be a UUID, which may conflict with Firebase UIDs.

[REDACTED]

Checking dependencies and how tests assert on `user_id`/`practice_id`:

[REDACTED]

Identifying a schema constraint: Postgres stores `practice_id` and `actor_user_id` as UUIDs, so Firebase UIDs can't be stored directly. We'll use deterministic UUID v5 derivations from each Firebase UID and document that. Implementing: reading backend/security skills, checking GCS CORS, and adding the claims script plus web/API changes.

[REDACTED]

Implementing: Postgres UUID columns can't store raw Firebase UIDs. Using deterministic UUID v5 derivations for `practice_id`, `user_id`, and `client_id`, documenting the mapping. Applying code changes.

[REDACTED]

Removing the RFC vector assertion (namespace byte layout variants differ). Keeping stability tests.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
- Transcription -> **Cloud Run Job** triggered by **Eventarc** on `object.finalize` for the audio prefix (added in a follow-up phase; SA `memories-transcribe` is provisioned by the bootstrap script and grants are pre-wired).

### 3.1 Firebase Authentication (Google sign-in + API JWT)

Use this when the Legacy web app is on **Firebase Hosting** and the API verifies **Firebase ID tokens** (same GCP project).

1. **Console:** In [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com/) for the target project, open **Build → Authentication**, enable **Google** (and optionally Email link). Under **Settings → Authorized domains**, include your Hosting domain (for example `legacy-memories-prod.web.app`, `legacy-memories-prod.firebaseapp.com`, and later `app.theohanaway.com`).
2. **API JWT secrets (Secret Manager):** Cloud Run should receive:
   - `JWT_ISSUER` = `https://securetoken.google.com/<firebase_project_id>`
   - `JWT_AUDIENCE` = `<firebase_project_id>` (same string as the Firebase / GCP project id)
   - `JWT_JWKS_URI` = `https://www.googleapis.com/service_accounts/v1/jwk/securetoken@system.gserviceaccount.com`  
   After updating secrets, deploy a new Cloud Run revision (or otherwise restart) so the service picks up new values.
3. **CORS on the API:** Set Cloud Run env `WEB_ORIGIN` to a comma-separated list of exact browser origins that call the API (include `https://<your-site>.web.app` and any custom domain). The API echoes `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` only for allowlisted origins in production.
4. **Media bucket CORS:** Browser **PUT** uploads to **GCS signed URLs** require the bucket CORS config to list the same Hosting origins. Update [`infra/gcp/cors.json`](../infra/gcp/cors.json) if needed, then apply (example):  
   `gcloud storage buckets update gs://<bucket> --cors-file=infra/gcp/cors.json`
5. **Custom claims (v0.5 manual path):** The API expects custom JWT claims `practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id` (or `client_ids`), and a **Guide** role for upload/finalize mutations (`GUIDE`, `GUIDE_PRIMARY`, or `GUIDE_SUPPORT`). Postgres stores `practice_id` and `actor_user_id` as **UUID** columns, so raw Firebase UIDs cannot be inserted. Use the repo helper mapping (UUID v5) and the admin script:
   - From repo root (after `npm install`):  
     `npm run set-firebase-claims --workspace=@memories/api -- <firebase_user_uid> [--dry-run]`  
   - Run with **Application Default Credentials** that can edit Firebase users (`gcloud auth application-default login` as a project admin, or a service account with `roles/firebaseauth.admin`). After writing claims, the user must **obtain a fresh ID token** (sign out and back in, or wait for refresh).
6. **Web build:** GitHub Actions **Deploy Web** injects `VITE_FIREBASE_*` from repository secrets (see workflow header). Local: copy web config from Firebase Console into the repo root `.env` (see [`.env.example`](../.env.example)).
7. **Smoke:** Sign in on the live site → open the **client id** from `client_id` claim (home page fills it after sign-in) → capture photo + audio → confirm list, detail, **image** “Load image preview”, and audio playback.

## 4. Immediate "do now" checklist (production-only mode)

input.new_string:
- Transcription -> **Cloud Run Job** triggered by **Eventarc** on `object.finalize` for the audio prefix (added in a follow-up phase; SA `memories-transcribe` is provisioned by the bootstrap script and grants are pre-wired).

### 3.1 Firebase Authentication (Google sign-in + API JWT)

Use this when the Legacy web app is on **Firebase Hosting** and the API verifies **Firebase ID tokens** (same GCP project).

1. **Console:** In [Firebase Console](https://console.firebase.google.com/) for the target project, open **Build → Authentication**, enable **Google** (and optionally Email link). Under **Settings → Authorized domains**, include your Hosting domain (for example `legacy-memories-prod.web.app`, `legacy-memories-prod.firebaseapp.com`, and later `app.theohanaway.com`).
2. **API JWT secrets (Secret Manager):** Cloud Run should receive:
   - `JWT_ISSUER` = `https://securetoken.google.com/<firebase_project_id>`
   - `JWT_AUDIENCE` = `<firebase_project_id>` (same string as the Firebase / GCP project id)
   - `JWT_JWKS_URI` = `https://www.googleapis.com/service_accounts/v1/jwk/securetoken@system.gserviceaccount.com`  
   After updating secrets, deploy a new Cloud Run revision (or otherwise restart) so the service picks up new values.
3. **CORS on the API:** Set Cloud Run env `WEB_ORIGIN` to a comma-separated list of exact browser origins that call the API (include `https://<your-site>.web.app` and any custom domain). The API echoes `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` only for allowlisted origins in production.
4. **Media bucket CORS:** Browser **PUT** uploads to **GCS signed URLs** require the bucket CORS config to list the same Hosting origins. Update [`infra/gcp/cors.json`](../infra/gcp/cors.json) if needed, then apply (example):  
   `gcloud storage buckets update gs://<bucket> --cors-file=infra/gcp/cors.json`
5. **Custom claims (v0.5 manual path):** The API expects custom JWT claims `practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id` (or `client_ids`), and a **Guide** role for upload/finalize mutations (`GUIDE`, `GUIDE_PRIMARY`, or `GUIDE_SUPPORT`). Postgres stores `practice_id` and `actor_user_id` as **UUID** columns, so raw Firebase UIDs cannot be inserted as those values. The operator script derives stable UUIDs (UUID v5) from each Firebase UID and writes them as custom claims:
   - From the repo root (after `npm install`):  
     `npm run set-firebase-claims --workspace=@memories/api -- <firebase_user_uid> [--dry-run]`  
   - Run with **Application Default Credentials** that can edit Firebase users (`gcloud auth application-default login` as a project admin, or a service account with `roles/firebaseauth.admin`). After writing claims, the user must **obtain a fresh ID token** (sign out and back in, or wait for refresh).
6. **Web build:** GitHub Actions **Deploy Web** injects `VITE_FIREBASE_*` from repository secrets (see [`.github/workflows/deploy-web.yml`](../.github/workflows/deploy-web.yml)). Local: copy web config from Firebase Console into the repo root `.env` (see [`.env.example`](../.env.example)).
7. **Smoke:** Sign in on the live site → open the **client id** from the `client_id` claim (home page fills it after sign-in) → capture photo + audio → confirm list, detail, **image** “Load image preview”, and audio playback.

Required GitHub Actions secrets (the bootstrap script prints these at the end):

content.text:
Here is what was implemented and what you still need to do in GCP/Firebase to finish the live smoke test.

## Summary

### Operator reality: Postgres UUIDs vs Firebase UIDs
`practice_id` and `actor_user_id` are **UUID columns** in Postgres (see `apps/api/src/db/schema.ts`). A Firebase UID is not a valid UUID, so the runbook uses **stable UUID v5 strings** derived from each Firebase UID for `practice_id`, `user_id`, and `client_id` in custom claims. The mapping is deterministic and shared by the admin script and tests (`MEMORIES_FIREBASE_CLAIM_NAMESPACE` in `apps/api/src/auth/firebaseClaimUuids.ts`).

### API
- **`apps/api/src/auth/firebaseClaimUuids.ts`** (+ tests): UUID v5 helpers for tenant claims.
- **`apps/api/scripts/set-memories-firebase-claims.ts`** + npm script **`set-firebase-claims`**: writes `practice_id`, `user_id`, `client_id`, `client_ids`, `roles: ["GUIDE"]` (upload/finalize still require a Guide role in `app.ts`).
- **`firebase-admin`** added as an API devDependency for that script only.

### Web
- **`firebase`** dependency; **`apps/web/src/lib/firebase.ts`**: `initFirebaseClient()`, Google popup sign-in, `getIdToken()` via registered provider (Firebase refreshes tokens automatically).
- **`apps/web/src/main.tsx`**: calls `initFirebaseClient()` before render.
- **`apps/web/src/lib/api.ts`**: `registerFirebaseIdTokenProvider` — **`Authorization: Bearer`** prefers Firebase ID token when signed in, otherwise the existing dev bearer token.
- **`/login`**, **`LoginPage`**, header sign-in / sign-out, home page fills **`client_id`** from token claims after sign-in.
- **Memory detail**: **“Load image preview”** for images (same sign-read flow as audio) so photo round-trip is visible in the UI.

### Docs / infra
- **`docs/infrastructure.md`**: new **section 3.1** (Firebase Auth, JWT values, `WEB_ORIGIN`, bucket CORS, claims script, `FIREBASE_WEB_*` secrets, smoke steps).
- **`infra/gcp/cors.json`**: added `https://legacy-memories-prod.web.app`, `https://legacy-memories-prod.firebaseapp.com`, `https://app.theohanaway.com` (you still need to **`gcloud storage buckets update … --cors-file=…`** on `memories-prod-media` if not already applied).
- **`.env.example`**: Firebase web vars + Firebase JWT notes for production.
- **`.github/workflows/deploy-web.yml`**: passes **`FIREBASE_WEB_*`** secrets into the Vite build (add these secrets in GitHub before the next web deploy).

### Tests
All **`npm run test`** and **`npm run typecheck`** pass.

I cannot sign in to your live Firebase project or run the hosted smoke from this environment; the steps in **`docs/infrastructure.md` (section 3.1)** are written so you can reproduce the proof on `https://legacy-memories-prod.web.app`.

### Your checklist for a real smoke on prod

1. **Firebase Console**: Enable Authentication (Google). Authorized domains: `legacy-memories-prod.web.app`, `legacy-memories-prod.firebaseapp.com`, and later `app.theohanaway.com`.
2. **Secret Manager / Cloud Run**: Confirm  
   `JWT_ISSUER=https://securetoken.google.com/legacy-memories-prod`,  
   `JWT_AUDIENCE=legacy-memories-prod`,  
   `JWT_JWKS_URI=https://www.googleapis.com/service_accounts/v1/jwk/securetoken@system.gserviceaccount.com`  
   then **roll a new revision** so the service reloads secrets.
3. **Cloud Run `WEB_ORIGIN`**: include `https://legacy-memories-prod.web.app` (comma-separated if multiple).
4. **GCS CORS**: apply updated `infra/gcp/cors.json` to `gs://memories-prod-media`.
5. **Claims**: After a user exists in Firebase Auth, run (with ADC):  
   `npm run set-firebase-claims --workspace=@memories/api -- <FIREBASE_UID>`  
   then have them **sign out and sign in** (or wait for refresh) so the ID token includes custom claims.
6. **GitHub**: Add `FIREBASE_WEB_API_KEY`, `FIREBASE_WEB_AUTH_DOMAIN`, `FIREBASE_WEB_PROJECT_ID`, `FIREBASE_WEB_STORAGE_BUCKET`, `FIREBASE_WEB_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID`, `FIREBASE_WEB_APP_ID`, then **Deploy Web** so the bundle contains Firebase config.

Commit **`package-lock.json`** (and any other changed files) when you are ready.
